what_it_does: Defines a browser-global ConceptV icon registry mapping icon names to inline SVG body strings, plus helper functions for creating SVG DOM elements or full inline SVG markup for a named icon.
observations: The file stores icons under window.CV_ICONS.data as string fragments rather than standalone SVG elements.
Icons are grouped by commented categories such as people, documents, communication, architecture, browser surfaces, actions, system status, logic flow, and platform-specific items.
The header documents a 24x24 grid, 1.5px default stroke, rounded line caps and joins, currentColor usage, and extension rules.
The rendering helpers wrap icon bodies in an SVG with shared viewBox, fill, stroke, stroke width, linecap, and linejoin attributes.
Unknown icon names throw an Error in both helper functions.
Some icons intentionally include filled shapes, text, polygons, or explicit fill/stroke overrides inside the stored body strings.
standouts: Large file-local icon vocabulary tailored to real estate, architecture, dashboards, documents, and platform controls.
The registry separates SVG body fragments from wrapper rendering concerns.
The public API supports both DOM SVG creation and string markup generation.
conventions: Uses a window.CV_ICONS browser global namespace.
Uses 24x24 SVG coordinates throughout the wrapper API.
Uses currentColor for tintable icon rendering.
Uses grouped section comments to organize icon categories.
Generally uses outline SVG geometry with rounded strokes, with local exceptions for filled marks and text glyphs.
concerns: The svg helper accepts a circle option but does not use it.
The usage comment mentions circle rendering, but no circle behavior is implemented in this file.
svg.innerHTML is populated from SVG body strings, which is acceptable for local static data but would be sensitive if icon entries became untrusted input.
Several icon bodies use text elements, so their appearance can depend on available fonts and SVG text rendering.
notes: This file is the browser-side icon registry and rendering helper for ConceptV's custom SVG icon set.
questions: What behavior was intended for the documented circle option?
Are the generated per-icon SVG files expected to be manually regenerated whenever this registry changes?
Should text-based icons remain text elements, or should they be converted to paths for rendering consistency?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it acts as a centralized ConceptV icon library and provides helper APIs for rendering named icons.
intention_signals: It seems intended to keep ConceptV icons consistent, editable in one registry, and reusable across React-style DOM creation, raw markup, and generated standalone SVG assets.
novelty: A project-specific SVG icon registry with domain-specific symbols for architecture, property, visualization, and platform workflows stored as reusable body fragments.
contribution: This file contributes a named, tintable SVG icon vocabulary and lightweight browser rendering utilities that a fused system would keep as a reusable visual substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Browser JavaScript file defining the ConceptV custom SVG icon registry and helper functions for rendering named 24x24 currentColor icons.
intention_for_embedding: This was for maintaining and reusing a consistent project-specific icon library across ConceptV interfaces and generated SVG assets.
